seoTitle: Macleay Island vs Magnolia — Climate Risk Comparison | ClimateNest
seoDescription: Compare climate risk scores between Macleay Island and Magnolia. See which suburb is safer from flood, bushfire, heat, and storm hazards.
ogTitle: Macleay Island vs Magnolia: Climate Safety
ogDescription: Data-driven comparison of climate risk in Macleay Island and Magnolia.
heading: Macleay Island vs Magnolia — Which is Safer?
intro: Compare climate risk between Macleay Island, Queensland and Magnolia, Queensland. Our analysis uses data from BOM, CSIRO, and state hazard authorities to score each suburb across 6 climate hazards.
verdict: Macleay Island has a lower overall climate risk score (37/100) compared to Magnolia (40/100). We recommend getting an address-level report for properties in both suburbs before making a purchase decision.

faq.question: Is Macleay Island or Magnolia safer from flooding?
faq.answer: Macleay Island has a flood risk score of 45/100, while Magnolia scores 53/100. A lower score indicates lower flood risk. Get an address-level flood risk report for a specific property.

faq.question: Which suburb has lower bushfire risk?
faq.answer: Macleay Island scores 30/100 for bushfire risk. Magnolia scores 52/100. Fire risk depends on proximity to vegetation and local fire history.

faq.question: Should I buy in Macleay Island or Magnolia?
faq.answer: Both suburbs have climate considerations. Macleay Island has a lower overall climate risk score. We recommend getting a full ClimateNest report for any specific property you're considering, which includes 8 hazard scores, insurance projections, and actionable recommendations.

valueImpact.description: Properties in this area may face a 1.52% climate value drag over time due to rising insurance costs and climate adaptation expenses.
